About: It is about gitano musical duo.
Affects: It affects subsequent nuevo flamenco releases.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with flamenco music.
It is associated with gitano musical duo.
It is associated with New flamenco.
It is associated with Romani families of artistic descent.
BelongsTo: It belongs to gitano musical duo.
ComposedBy: It was composed by Dolores Montoya Rodríguez and Manuel Molina Jiménez.
ConsistsOf: It consists of singer Dolores Montoya Rodríguez and guitarist Manuel Molina Jiménez.
CreatedBy: It was created by Dolores Montoya Rodríguez and Manuel Molina Jiménez.
HasA: It has emotive, yet sweet voice of Lole Montoya.
It has operatic, flashy guitar of Manuel Molina Jiménez.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by innovative flamenco music.
It is characterized by the emotive, yet sweet voice of Lole Montoya.
It is characterized by the operatic, flashy guitar of Manuel Molina Jiménez.
It is characterized by being the first exponent of flamenco music aimed at a non-exclusively flamenco audience.
It is characterized by being one of the precursors to the musical stream called New flamenco.
It is characterized by fused traditional Spanish flamenco with Molina's poetic lyrics and foreign styles and influences.
It is characterized by incorporating Arabic influences.
It is characterized by orchestral arrangements.
It is characterized by symphonic versions of Manuel de Falla's works.
Influenced: It influenced subsequent nuevo flamenco releases, including Veneno and La leyenda del tiempo.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Molina's poetic lyrics.
It was influenced by foreign styles and influences.
IsA: It is a gitano musical duo.
It is an exponent of flamenco music.
It is a precursor to the musical stream called New flamenco.
KnownFor: It is known for innovative flamenco music.
It is known for the emotive, yet sweet voice of Lole Montoya.
It is known for the operatic, flashy guitar of Manuel Molina Jiménez.
It is known for being the first exponent of flamenco music aimed at a non-exclusively flamenco audience.
It is known for being one of the precursors to the musical stream called New flamenco.
PartOf: It is part of gitano musical duo.
PerformedBy: It was performed by Dolores Montoya Rodríguez and Manuel Molina Jiménez.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Ricardo Pachón.
It was produced by Imán and Califato Independiente.
It was produced by Gualberto García.
PublishedAs: It was published as Nuevo Día.
It was published as Pasaje del Agua.
It was published as Lole y Manuel.
It was published as Al Alba Con Alegría.
It was published as Lole y Manuel Cantan a Manuel de Falla.
It was published as Alba Molina.
It was published as Una Voz y Una Guitarra.
UsedAs: It is used as a soundtrack for Quentin Tarantino's Kill Bill: Volume 2.
UsedFor: It is used for flamenco music.
It is used for innovative flamenco music.
It is used for New flamenco.
It is used for fused traditional Spanish flamenco with Molina's poetic lyrics and foreign styles and influences.
It is used for experimental style.
It is used for symphonic versions of Manuel de Falla's works.
It is used for a tribute to their daughter Alba Molina.
UsedIn: It is used in the 2018 documentary film Camarón: Flamenco y Revolución.
It is used in Kill Bill: Volume 2.
UsedTo: It is used to fuse traditional Spanish flamenco with Molina's poetic lyrics and foreign styles and influences.
It is used to incorporate Arabic influences.
It is used to feature orchestral arrangements.
It is used to create symphonic versions of Manuel de Falla's works.
WrittenBy: It was written by Juan Manuel Flores.
